journey.name: Planhat Customer Success Platform Journey

stages.name: Problem Recognition
stages.description: B2B SaaS companies realize their customer success efforts are reactive and they're losing customers silently without warning signs
stages.topFailureRisk: Generic positioning that doesn't address the specific pain of silent churn
stages.successPattern: Lead with outcome-focused messaging: 'Companies like yours prevent 30% of churn by detecting at-risk customers 90 days earlier'
stages.benchmarkContext: B2B SaaS companies typically lose 20-30% of prospects at this stage due to unclear value proposition

meaningfulMoments.name: Silent Churn Realization
meaningfulMoments.description: Prospect discovers they've lost customers without any warning signals or ability to intervene
recommendations: Position Planhat as early warning system
Share silent churn prevention case studies
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Prospects need to see the cost of reactive customer success before they value proactive solutions
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Create content showing the true cost of silent churn with specific dollar calculations
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Track content engagement to identify prospects actively researching churn prevention
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Prospects won't understand urgency and will delay purchase decisions indefinitely

stages.name: Solution Evaluation
stages.description: Prospects compare Planhat against Gainsight, Totango, and ChurnZero while building internal consensus
stages.topFailureRisk: Getting commoditized in feature comparisons instead of differentiated on outcomes
stages.successPattern: Champion enablement kit with competitive differentiation focused on AI-powered automation vs manual processes
stages.benchmarkContext: Customer success platform evaluations typically involve 6-8 stakeholders and take 4-8 weeks

meaningfulMoments.name: Competitor Comparison
meaningfulMoments.description: Prospect evaluates Planhat against established players like Gainsight with larger feature sets
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Prospects default to feature checklists instead of evaluating which platform prevents churn fastest
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Create head-to-head comparison showing time-to-first-insight: Planhat vs competitors
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Post-demo survey asking which specific capability addresses their biggest pain point
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Deals stall in evaluation as prospects get overwhelmed by feature comparisons

meaningfulMoments.name: Stakeholder Buy-in
meaningfulMoments.description: Champion needs to sell Planhat internally to finance, IT, and executive leadership
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Champions struggle to articulate Planhat's value to stakeholders who care about different outcomes
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Create stakeholder-specific one-pagers: ROI for finance, security for IT, growth metrics for executives
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Track deal velocity by stakeholder involvement to identify enablement gaps
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Deals stall as champions can't build consensus without proper enablement materials

stages.name: Demo & Trial
stages.description: Prospects see Planhat in action using their actual customer data to demonstrate health scoring and churn prevention
stages.topFailureRisk: Generic demo showing all features instead of solving their specific churn problem
stages.successPattern: Value-first demo design: show health score identifying at-risk customers in first 10 minutes
stages.benchmarkContext: B2B SaaS demos convert 20-30% to next step when properly customized

meaningfulMoments.name: Health Score Aha Moment
meaningfulMoments.description: Prospect sees how Planhat would have predicted their recent churn cases 60-90 days earlier
recommendations: Use their actual churned customers as examples
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Prospects need to see their specific problem solved, not generic platform capabilities
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Prepare demo using prospect's actual customer scenarios showing churn prevention timeline
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Post-demo CES survey measuring how clearly they understood the solution to their problem
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Generic demos fail to create conviction and deals lose momentum immediately

stages.name: Negotiation
stages.description: Prospect has chosen Planhat and is optimizing terms, timeline, and implementation approach
stages.topFailureRisk: Price objections because value wasn't sufficiently anchored before pricing discussion
stages.successPattern: Outcome-based value narrative: frame investment against cost of silent churn they're currently experiencing
stages.benchmarkContext: Customer success platform deals typically close 15-25% below list price

meaningfulMoments.name: ROI Justification
meaningfulMoments.description: Prospect needs to justify Planhat investment to finance team using specific churn prevention numbers
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Finance teams evaluate cost without context of current churn losses being prevented
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Create ROI calculator showing annual churn cost vs Planhat investment with payback timeline
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Track pricing objection frequency to identify value communication gaps
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Deals get stuck in procurement without clear ROI justification for investment

stages.name: Implementation
stages.description: Customer integrates Planhat with their systems and sets up initial health scoring rules
stages.topFailureRisk: Technical setup delays that prevent reaching first value within 48 hours
stages.successPattern: First value in 48 hours: health score running on their customer data showing immediate insights
stages.benchmarkContext: Customer success platforms typically take 2-4 weeks for full implementation

meaningfulMoments.name: Data Integration Success
meaningfulMoments.description: Customer's data is flowing into Planhat and health scores are calculating for their customer base
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Technical setup success determines whether customer sees value fast enough to build usage habits
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Send personalized milestone celebration when first 100 customer health scores are generated
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Implementation progress tracking with automated milestone celebrations
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Delayed value realization leads to buyer's remorse and implementation stall

stages.name: Early Adoption
stages.description: Customer team learns to interpret health scores and begins taking action on at-risk customer alerts
stages.topFailureRisk: Customer success team overwhelmed by alerts without clear prioritization or action playbooks
stages.successPattern: Proactive Day 7 check-in with prioritized action recommendations based on their top at-risk customers
stages.benchmarkContext: 60% of customer success platform users abandon adoption if they don't see churn prevention within 30 days

meaningfulMoments.name: First Churn Save
meaningfulMoments.description: Customer prevents their first churn using Planhat's early warning system
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: First churn prevention success proves ROI and builds confidence in the system
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Create case study template for first churn save with specific dollar impact calculation
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Churn prevention tracking with automated win documentation
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Without early wins, customer questions investment and usage drops off

stages.name: Value Realization
stages.description: Customer can quantify churn reduction and ROI from using Planhat's predictive capabilities
stages.topFailureRisk: Customer can't articulate specific ROI metrics at renewal time
stages.successPattern: Quarterly Business Review documenting churn prevented, revenue saved, and team productivity gains
stages.benchmarkContext: Customer success platform ROI typically becomes measurable after 6 months of consistent usage

meaningfulMoments.name: ROI Measurement
meaningfulMoments.description: Customer calculates definitive ROI from churn prevention and can defend Planhat investment
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Customers who can't articulate ROI at renewal time become price-sensitive and churn risks
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Run QBR showing: customers at-risk identified, churn prevented, revenue saved, team efficiency gains
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Quarterly NPS with ROI articulation follow-up questions
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Renewal becomes price negotiation instead of value continuation

stages.name: Expansion
stages.description: Customer adds more team members, higher plan tiers, or additional Planhat modules
stages.topFailureRisk: Missing expansion signals when customer is ready to grow usage
stages.successPattern: Proactive expansion alerts when customers hit usage limits or request advanced features
stages.benchmarkContext: Customer success platforms typically see 15-25% net revenue expansion among successful customers

meaningfulMoments.name: Usage Limit Signals
meaningfulMoments.description: Customer approaches plan limits or requests features from higher tiers
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Customers showing expansion signals are ready to invest more but need proactive outreach
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: Set automated alerts for usage thresholds and feature requests indicating upgrade readiness
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: Expansion signal detection with automated account review triggers
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Customers may seek alternatives rather than upgrade if expansion isn't offered proactively

stages.name: Renewal & Advocacy
stages.description: Customer renews Planhat subscription and potentially becomes reference customer for similar prospects
stages.topFailureRisk: Surprise renewal objections because customer health wasn't monitored pre-renewal
stages.successPattern: Pre-renewal value summary 60 days before renewal showing quantified churn prevention outcomes
stages.benchmarkContext: Customer success platform renewal rates typically range from 85-95% for successful implementations

meaningfulMoments.name: Advocacy Moment
meaningfulMoments.description: Customer expresses genuine satisfaction and willingness to recommend Planhat to peers
meaningfulMoments.diagnosis: Peak satisfaction moments create narrow windows for advocacy requests
meaningfulMoments.actionTemplate: When customer gives NPS 9-10, immediately follow up asking for specific testimonial or referral
meaningfulMoments.cxToolRecommendation: NPS trigger for immediate advocacy outreach automation
meaningfulMoments.impactIfIgnored: Advocacy opportunities fade quickly if not captured at peak satisfaction moments

confrontationInsights.description: You're probably losing 2-3% of customers monthly without any warning system - that's the industry average for B2B SaaS your size
confrontationInsights.businessImpact: At $4.2M ARR with 2% monthly churn, you're losing $84K monthly in silent churn
confrontationInsights.immediateAction: Interview your last 5 churned customers to understand how early the warning signs appeared
confrontationInsights.measureWith: Post-churn exit survey asking 'when did you first consider leaving?'
confrontationInsights.companionAdvice: I've seen this movie before - the churn you see is just the tip of the iceberg

confrontationInsights.description: Without health scoring, your CS team is flying blind - reacting to problems instead of preventing them
confrontationInsights.businessImpact: Companies with health scores reduce churn 20-30% by catching at-risk customers 60-90 days earlier
confrontationInsights.immediateAction: Build a simple health score with 4 inputs: login frequency, feature usage, support tickets, payment status
confrontationInsights.measureWith: Track how many at-risk customers you identify before they complain vs after
confrontationInsights.companionAdvice: You're in the customer success platform business - you need your own health score first

confrontationInsights.description: Your demos probably show features instead of solving specific churn problems - that's why conversion feels low
confrontationInsights.businessImpact: Value-first demos convert 2-3x better than feature tour demos for B2B SaaS
confrontationInsights.immediateAction: Restructure demos to show health score preventing their actual churn cases in first 10 minutes
confrontationInsights.measureWith: Post-demo survey asking 'how clearly did we address your specific challenge?'
confrontationInsights.companionAdvice: Stop showing what your product does - start showing what their problem looks like solved

cxToolRoadmap.whenToDeploy: Immediately for your own customer base
cxToolRoadmap.whyThisTool: You need to eat your own dog food - build the health score you sell to customers
cxToolRoadmap.expectedOutcome: Identify 20-30% of at-risk customers 60-90 days earlier

cxToolRoadmap.whenToDeploy: After every demo starting next week
cxToolRoadmap.whyThisTool: Measure whether demos solve specific problems vs just show features
cxToolRoadmap.expectedOutcome: Identify which demo elements create conviction vs confusion

cxToolRoadmap.whyThisTool: Connect satisfaction scores to quantifiable churn prevention outcomes
cxToolRoadmap.expectedOutcome: Build renewal value narratives before you need them

impactProjections.calculation: 350 customers × $12K ACV × 2% monthly churn × 20-30% reduction × 12 months
Assuming 50 demos/month × 20% current conversion × 30-60% improvement × $12K ACV

techStackRecommendations.whyNow: You need your own health scoring system to prevent silent churn
techStackRecommendations.connectWith: Your CRM and product analytics to create comprehensive customer health pictures

techStackRecommendations.whyNow: Start with simple CSAT and CES surveys to understand experience gaps
techStackRecommendations.connectWith: Your CRM to close the feedback loop and track sentiment trends

techStackRecommendations.whyNow: Usage patterns are the strongest predictor of churn for SaaS platforms
techStackRecommendations.connectWith: Your health score system to create behavioral churn prediction models

assumptions: Estimated 350 customers and $12K ACV based on industry benchmarks for 51-150 employee B2B SaaS companies
Assumed 2% monthly churn rate typical for growing-stage customer success platforms
Used benchmark data since no specific customer metrics were provided
journey.maturityAssessment: Dana, you're at a classic inflection point - you've built a great product but you're flying blind on your own customer health. The irony is thick: you sell churn prevention but don't have early warning systems for your own customers.

onboardingData.customerDescription: B2B SaaS companies needing customer success platform
onboardingData.biggestChallenge: Customers churn silently — we only find out when they're already gone

enrichmentData.description: Planhat is an AI-powered customer platform that automates and augments commercial operations for sales, customer success, and professional services teams.
enrichmentData.reasoning: Planhat is a well-known B2B SaaS company in the customer success platform space, competing directly with Gainsight and Totango.
